"flipboard.app", "enabled": true, "bounds": [0, 1972, -34, 2198], "pressed": "not_pressed", "scrollable-vertical": false}, {"resource-id": "flipboard.app:id/post_item_narrow_excerpt", "adapter-view": false, "pointer": "b58b6f1", "scrollable-horizontal": false, "ancestors": ["android.view.View", "java.lang.Object"], "selected": false, "content-desc": ["(CNN) \u2014 Former astronaut and US Sen. John Glenn, 95, was hospitalized \"more than a week ago,\" according to Ohio State University spokesman Hank Wilson.\n\"Sen. Glenn is at The James Cancer Hospital at The Ohio State University (and) was admitted there more than a week ago,\" Wilson wrote in an email. \"I do not know his condition or illness or prognosis. I caution that even though Sen. Glenn is at The James that does not necessarily mean he has cancer.\"\nGlenn had heart valve replacement surgery in 2014.\nHe became the first American to orbit the Earth in 1962.\nGlenn piloted the Mercury space capsule, dubbed Friendship 7, and circled the planet three times in just under five hours on February 20, 1962. Of the original seven US astronauts who made up Project Mercury -- Glenn, Alan Shepard, Gus Grissom, Gordon Cooper, Scott Carpenter, Walter Schirra and Donald Slayton -- Glenn is the last surviving member.\nPrior to his career as an astronaut, Glenn flew 149 missions during World War II and the Korean War and received multiple medals and decorations, including the Distinguished Flying Cross on six occasions.\nHe resigned from the astronaut program in 1964 and pursued a career in politics, serving as a US senator as a Democrat from Ohio between 1974 and 1999. He even ran for president in 1984. But Glenn's time in space wasn't over.\nAt 77, he became the oldest person to ever travel in space. Glenn was a payload specialist aboard the space shuttle Discovery for a nine-day mission in 1998.\nIn 2011, he received a Congressional Gold Medal alongside Neil Armstrong, Buzz Aldrin and Michael Collins. In 2012, he received the Presidential Medal of Freedom from President Obama.\nThis year, he attended a celebration that saw the renaming of Port Columbus Airport to John Glenn Columbus International Airport.\nGlenn and his wife, Annie, have been married for 73 years. "flipboard.app:id/excerpt", "adapter-view": false, "pointer": "bee16ae", "scrollable-horizontal": false, "ancestors": ["android.view.View", "java.lang.Object"], "selected": false, "content-desc": ["We could soon be talking about Apple in the same way we talk about Redbox or Netflix \u2014 new technology that cannibalized a long-held standard.\nCiting sources familiar with the matter, a report from Bloomberg says Apple is applying tremendous pressure to Hollywood studios for earlier access to movies. In the US, a blockbuster film tends to have a theater run of two months or more before coming to home video via streaming service, Blu-ray, or DVD.\nApple could soon allow iTunes users to rent the same films at home as early as two weeks after their theater release.\n21st Century Fox, Warner Bros., and Universal Pictures, have all confirmed they are looking at high-priced rentals of new movies shortly after their release. It\u2019s not a done deal, but Apple\u2019s iTunes platform seems to check a lot of boxes for the studios and it\u2019s already in millions of homes.\nAccording to Warner Bros. chief Kevin Tsujihara, one option is a premium-priced rental services that would release new movies at a hefty price tag of $25 to $50 each. A small family would argue that this is still beneficial to theater pricing, and doesn\u2019t require leaving the house with small children.\nMillions are already foregoing theater chains due to cost, relatively quick availability of new titles after their theater run, and of course, convenience. The industry isn\u2019t necessarily dying, yet, but it\u2019s certainly in trouble. Smaller theaters are being swallowed by larger ones, and the big three \u2014 AMC, Regal, and Cinemark \u2014 aren\u2019t exactly innovating aside from finding new ways to empty our pockets. If successful, Apple\u2019s move would almost certainly kill the traditional cinema.\nApple Is in Talks With Hollywood for Early Access to Movies on iTunes on Bloomberg\nAppleTech"], "rel-bounds": "ab1fe4b", "scrollable-horizontal": false, "ancestors": ["android.view.View", "java.lang.Object"], "selected": false, "content-desc": ["As cannabis use becomes normalized, the tech industry is finally starting to innovate the seed to weed growing process.\nA venture capital-backed company called LEAF just announced a new \"Plug-N-Plant\" grow system on Wednesday, making it easier for consumers to grow their own pesticide-free pot at home without having to maintain it like you would growing it the old fashioned way.\nRecreational marijuana use is now legal in eight states and Washington, D.C., thanks to the recent election, and medical cannabis is allowed in some form in 29 states, returning cannabis as a major cash crop for the United States. In many of these areas and all states where cannabis has been legalized, you're allowed to grow your own pot for your own personal use, but growing weed has its fair share of headaches.\nBranded as a smart home appliance, LEAF is an app-connected system that allows you to control various aspects of the growing process. Using the iOS or Android app, the self-contained system will let you control the temperature, lighting, humidity, nutrient dosing and pH balance. Additionally, the system can hook up to your home's water and sewage systems for automated watering. No longer will you kill your plant by forgetting to water it or by accidental drowning.\n\"We have industrial grade sensors closely and accurately monitoring every aspect of the grow: air temp, air humidity, plant height, water TDS, water pH, water temp, water level,\" founder and LEAF CEO Yoni Ofir tells Mashable. \"These sensors work together to provide data about everything going on in the grow, and then relays that to the the LEAF cloud system for constant analysis. When something is out of line, the system knows to correct it immediately. For example, dosing pH down if the pH rises above a threshold within that grow recipe.\"\nIn terms of appearance, since the system will be located in your home, the LEAF looks like a small, apartment-sized refrigerator, which is a lot better than a series of fluorescent lights hanging from the ceiling in your spare room. Growing cannabis needs absolute darkness for up to 12 hours during certain times of the cycle, so the enclosure is perfect for those living in small spaces.\nAdditionally, a carbon filter helps contain some of the pungent odor the cannabis plant is known for, which is great for those living with nosy neighbors close by. \"You can be in a closed room with the unit and you won't smell anything funky,\" Ofir said.\nWhile this is definitely not the first automated \u2026"], "rel-bounds": [35, 1616, 35, 1616], "draw": false, "focusable": false, "long-clickable": false, "visibility": "visible", [{"resource-id": "flipboard.app:id/excerpt", "adapter-view": false, "pointer": "3f9fb5f", "scrollable-horizontal": false, "ancestors": ["android.view.View", "java.lang.Object"], "selected": false, "content-desc": ["After a fair bit of talk about getting into mobile games this year, Sony has finally announced that the company is working on a total of 10 mobile gaming titles, six of which are expected to be released in 2017. The 10 titles are to be based on classic Sony IP and look set to include PaRappa the Rapper and Arc The Lad.\nSony\u2019s mobile games will initially be released in Japan and will then be followed up be dates in other Asian countries. It\u2019s not clear if Sony intends to bring any of these games to the West in an attempt to capitalize on the success of games like Pokemon Go. There\u2019s some extra digging required to find all of the upcoming titles, as Sony didn\u2019t announcement all of them officially at the event, but the company\u2019s ForwardWorks studio has announced a list that contains most of them. Unfortunately most of the release dates are still to be determined, but here\u2019s the list of 10:\nMingol\n\u2022\u00a0Hot Shots Golf\n\u2022\u00a0No Heroes Allowed! DASH!\n\u2022\u00a0Arc The Lad Wild Arms\n\u2022\u00a0Doko Demo Issho\n\u2022\u00a0PaRappa the Rapper\n\u2022\u00a0Boku no Natsuyasumi\n\u2022\u00a0Disgaea\n\u2022\u00a0Yomawari\n\u2022\u00a0Sora to Umi no Aida\nThese upcoming titles look set to be released by the company\u2019s ForwardWorks development studio. Sony setup ForwardWorks in Toyko back at the end of March and the studio will be working exclusively on mobile titles mostly using PlayStation IP. Square Enix is also going to work in collaboration with ForwardWorks on a title that will be announced at a later date.\nSony\u2019s move into mobile games should help relieve some of the pressure that it\u2019s facing from this growing gaming segment, which is particularly big business in the company\u2019s home country of Japan. In Japan, mobile gaming now accounts for more than half of the $12.4 billion market, according to research firm Newzoo."], "rel-bounds": [35, 849, 35, 849], "draw": false, "focusable": false,
